cedi: noun. The currency of Ghana, divided into 100 pesewas and represented by ₵.
ceil: verb. To line or finish (a surface, such as a wall), with plaster, stucco, thin boards, or similar.
cel: noun. A piece of celluloid on which has been drawn a frame of an animated film.
cline: noun. A gradation in a character or phenotype within a species, deme, or other systematic group.
del: noun. The symbol ∇ used to denote the gradient operator.
deli: noun. A shop that sells cooked or prepared food ready for serving.
den: noun. A small cavern or hollow place in the side of a hill, or among rocks; especially, a cave used by a wild animal for shelter or
dice: noun. Gaming with one or more dice.
die: verb. To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death. followed by of as an indication of direct cause; general use:
diel: adjective. Having a 24-hour period regardless of day or night.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
dine: verb. To eat; to eat dinner or supper.
eld: noun. One's age, age in years, period of life.
end: noun. The terminal point of something in space or time.
incline: verb. To bend or move (something) out of a given plane or direction, often the horizontal or vertical.
inclined: adjective. At an angle to the horizontal; slanted or sloped.
indie: adjective. Independent; from outside the mainstream.
inn: noun. Any establishment where travellers can procure lodging, food, and drink.
inned: verb. simple past and past participle of inn
led: verb. simple past and past participle of lead
lei: noun. A garland of flowers in Hawaii.
lend: verb. To allow to be used by someone temporarily, on condition that it or its equivalent will be returned.
lid: noun. The top or cover of a container.
lie: verb. To rest in a horizontal position on a surface.
lied: noun. An art song, usually sung solo in German and accompanied on the piano.
lin: verb. To desist, to stop, to cease.
line: noun. A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
lined: adjective. Having a lining, an inner layer or covering.
linen: noun. Thread or cloth made from flax fiber.
linin: noun. The network of viscous material in a cell's nucleus that connects the chromatin granules.
linn: noun. A waterfall or cataract (torrent of water running over a rocky bed), or a ravine down which such a waterfall rushes.
nine: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 9.
ceili: noun. A social event with traditional Irish or Scottish music and dancing.
cid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chimariko.
clied: verb. simple past and past participle of cly
clinid: noun. Any fish in the family Clinidae.
dei: interjection. Alternative spelling of dey (“informal term of address used when trying to get someone's attention.”).
deni: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to 0.01 Macedonian denar.
dinic: adjective. Relating to vertigo or dizziness.
dle: noun. A short, daily web-based guessing game or puzzle, especially one of many played one after another.
eli: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nding.
ide: noun. A freshwater fish of the family Cyprinidae, found across northern Europe and Asia, especially Leuciscus idus.
incide: verb. To separate and remove (something); to cut.
indin: noun. A dark red crystalline substance, isomeric with and resembling indigo blue, and obtained from isatide and dioxindol.
lienic: adjective. Of or pertaining to the spleen.
lind: noun. The lime tree, or linden tree.
linned: verb. simple past and past participle of lin
ned: noun. A person, usually a youth, of low social standing and education, a violent disposition and with a particular style of dress
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
nne: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngandyera.
